mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses predictive power during non-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days specifically during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration predicts negative or flat 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or regime-split tests show no directional advantage over baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms sustained economic activity and wage income expansion, supporting corporate earnings expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 2.5% annually forces monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, raising borrowing costs and compressing equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 2.5% YoY coincides with positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales validates broad economic demand expansion, reinforcing corporate profit forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth yields no predictive edge for 20-day returns, or joint positive signals produce negative returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing contraction, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal performs identically across early and late cycle phases.
